**Wingate** is a village in [County Durham](/wiki/County_Durham "County Durham"), England.

Wingate is a former [pit village](/wiki/Pit_village "Pit village") with a mixture of 19th-century, post-war, and more recent housing developments. It was originally inhabited by around 30 farmers before 1839 when coal was discovered. It is located in the East of County Durham, three miles south west of [Peterlee](/wiki/Peterlee "Peterlee"), and seven miles north west of [Hartlepool](/wiki/Hartlepool "Hartlepool"). As with most villages in the area, it grew rapidly with the development of coal-mining in the region.

The name Wingate is said to derive from the [Anglo-Saxon](/wiki/Old_English_language "Old English language") words *windig* (windy) and *geat* (road) meaning windy road. Like many County Durham villages, residents are known to speak the [pitmatic](/wiki/Pitmatic "Pitmatic") dialect, described to be a mixture of both mackem and teesside accents, although new housing developments has seen a sharp increase in the village's population.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wingate,_County_Durham&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

There is no evidence of settlement at Wingate until the 16th century. However, mining made its presence felt in the 19th century and Wingate became a large settlement and regional centre for the area. The village is located approximately two miles east of the original settlement, which is now called [Old Wingate](/wiki/Old_Wingate "Old Wingate"). Coal was discovered in 1839 when two shafts were sunk; coal was drawn in December 1839. In 1906, an explosion in the mine killed 26 pit workers in Wingate, and in 2006 a march took place to commemorate the miners.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On [7 January 1971](/wiki/January_1971#January_7.2C_1971_.28Thursday.29 "January 1971"), [Avro Vulcan](/wiki/Avro_Vulcan "Avro Vulcan") bomber XM610 crashed near the school after suffering an engine fire due to metal fatigue in the number 1 engine. The pilot remained in the burning aircraft before he ejected to direct the aircraft to crash into the sea; however. the aircraft later spiralled down into the village and crashed, leaving a large crater. There were no fatalities in the accident. The pilot was awarded the [AFC](/wiki/Air_Force_Cross_(United_Kingdom) "Air Force Cross (United Kingdom)") for his attempts to save the aircraft.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Wingate is also the birthplace of [Ted Harrison](/wiki/Ted_Harrison "Ted Harrison"), a [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") artist notable for his paintings of the [Yukon](/wiki/Yukon "Yukon").[[4]](#cite_note-amos-4)[[5]](#cite_note-cbc.ca-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Governance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wingate,_County_Durham&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Governance")]

An [electoral ward](/wiki/Wards_and_electoral_divisions_of_the_United_Kingdom "Wards and electoral divisions of the United Kingdom") in the same name exists. This ward stretches north east to [Peterlee](/wiki/Peterlee "Peterlee") and has a total population, taken at the 2011 census, of 10,302.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wingate,_County_Durham&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Transport")]

### Car travel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wingate,_County_Durham&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Car travel")]

The village has ready access to road links for [commuters](/wiki/Commuters "Commuters"), with the A181 leading westward to [Durham](/wiki/Durham,_England "Durham, England") and then north and south via the [A1](/wiki/A1_road_(Great_Britain) "A1 road (Great Britain)"). Heading east towards the east Durham coast leads to the [A19](/wiki/A19_road "A19 road"), with links to [Peterlee](/wiki/Peterlee "Peterlee"), [Sunderland](/wiki/Sunderland,_Tyne_and_Wear "Sunderland, Tyne and Wear") and [Teesside](/wiki/Teesside "Teesside"). The main road through the village is the B1280.

### Public transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wingate,_County_Durham&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Public transport")]

Public transport is provided by [Arriva North East](/wiki/Arriva_North_East "Arriva North East") and [Go North East](/wiki/Go_North_East "Go North East"). Buses serve a range of destinations including: [Sedgefield](/wiki/Sedgefield "Sedgefield"), [Durham](/wiki/Durham,_England "Durham, England"), [Hartlepool](/wiki/Hartlepool "Hartlepool"), [Middlesbrough](/wiki/Middlesbrough "Middlesbrough"), [Peterlee](/wiki/Peterlee "Peterlee") and [Stockton-on-Tees](/wiki/Stockton-on-Tees "Stockton-on-Tees")

Wingate was previously served by two railway stations at [Wingate](/w/index.php?title=Wingate_(County_Durham)_railway_station&action=edit&redlink=1 "Wingate (County Durham) railway station (page does not exist)") and [Wellfield](/wiki/Wellfield_railway_station "Wellfield railway station") on the [Great North of England, Clarence & Hartlepool Junction Railway](/wiki/Hartlepool_Dock_%26_Railway "Hartlepool Dock & Railway") and the [North Eastern Railway](/wiki/North_Eastern_Railway_(UK) "North Eastern Railway (UK)") [Castle Eden Branch](/wiki/Castle_Eden_Railway "Castle Eden Railway") respectively. Both stations are long closed and the majority of the former right of way has been reclaimed and turned into walkways, notably parts of the Hart to Haswell Walkway. One of the level crossing gates survives next to the Railway Crossings pub on Front Street.

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wingate,_County_Durham&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Wellfield School](/wiki/Wellfield_School "Wellfield School"), in Wingate
